sourceMovie.title: Ted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ted for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Seth MacFarlane keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ted 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ted clicked for you, Ted 2 (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ted?
faq.answer: Try Ted 2, Ruby Sparks, The Science of Sleep—each shares DNA with Ted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ted?
faq.answer: Ted (2012) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ted part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ted Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
